_comment: CANONICAL SIGHASH GOLDEN for a fixed covenant spend. Pins the backend calc_schnorr_signature_hash (vendored kaspa-consensus-core) over one deterministic native-subnetwork P2SH spend. Asserted by the Rust test sighash_vector_matches_committed_golden in backend/src/covenant_builder.rs. A later frontend task asserts the browser kaspa-wasm sighash over the SAME fixed spend equals expected_sighash_hex, making this the canonical Rust<->browser interop anchor. See docs/SIGHASH_PARITY.md for the native+empty-payload divergence (this vector deliberately uses the NON-empty payload both stacks agree on).
_generation: Deterministic, no randomness. spend.redeem_hex = redeem_singlesig(redeem_singlesig_xonly) (the same fixed xonly as tests/fixtures/redeem_golden.json). The spent UTXO script_public_key is p2sh_script_pubkey(redeem) (the P2SH wrapper, which is what a P2SH sighash commits to). The tx is Transaction::new_non_finalized(version=0, [one input], [one output], lock_time, SubnetworkId native all-zero, gas, payload) with payload = the backend non-empty spend marker bytes "covex-p2sh-spend". expected_sighash_hex = hex(calc_schnorr_signature_hash(signable.as_verifiable(), 0, SIG_HASH_ALL)) emitted by the test itself; to regenerate, edit the spend fields, run the test once to read the asserted-vs-got diff, and paste the got value here.
